topicName: Shapes Around Us - 3D Shapes, Faces, Edges, Vertices, Angles
description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for geometry: 3D shapes in daily life, faces/edges/vertices, understanding angles, and identifying shapes around us

excellent_performance.rationale: Student has excellent 2D shape knowledge and can fast-track to 3D shapes, properties, and angles with challenges

videoSequence.reason: Introduction to 3D shapes found in daily life
Learn faces, edges, and vertices of 3D shapes
Recognize shapes in the real world
Fun 3D shapes song as a challenge review

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of 3D shapes, properties, angles, and real-world identification

videoSequence.reason: Learn common 3D shapes with real-life examples
Reinforce 3D shape names with a catchy song
Discover faces, edges, and vertices of solid shapes
Understand angle basics: right, acute, obtuse, straight
Fun angles song with real-world examples

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in 2D shapes or counting sides, needs targeted review before 3D shapes and angles

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Basic 2D shapes - circles, triangles, rectangles, squares
Review: Counting sides and corners of shapes
Start main topic: 3D shapes in daily life
Faces, edges, and vertices of 3D shapes

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant prerequisite review in 2D shapes, sides, and flat vs solid before attempting 3D shapes and angles

videoSequence.reason: Foundation: Learn to recognize and name 2D shapes
Essential: Learn to count sides and corners
Critical: Understand the difference between flat and solid shapes
Gentle intro to 3D shapes in daily life
Reinforce 3D shapes with a fun song

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these review videos, try the readiness quiz again to check if you are ready to continue with faces, edges, vertices, and angles.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

counting_sides.conceptName: Counting sides and corners of shapes

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: Which 3D shape looks like a ball?
questions.explanation: A sphere is the 3D shape that looks like a ball. It is perfectly round from every direction.

questions.question: A dice is shaped like a:
questions.explanation: A dice is a cube. It has 6 square faces, all the same size.

ifAllCorrect.message: Great job! You understand 3D shapes really well.

ifAllWrong.reason: Student struggling with recognizing basic 3D shapes

questions.question: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.explanation: A cube has 6 faces. Think of a dice - it has numbers on 6 sides, so 6 faces!

ifCorrect.message: Well done! You understand faces of 3D shapes.

ifWrong.message: Try counting the faces on a real box or dice before continuing to angles.

skip_ahead.action: Skip to identifying shapes around us for enrichment challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on 2D shapes and counting sides

suggest_break.trigger: After 4 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend finding 3D shapes at home and counting their faces, edges, and corners

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You know your shapes very well. You are ready to explore 3D shapes, count faces and edges, learn about angles, and spot shapes everywhere around you. We have created a fast-track playlist with some fun challenges!

good_score.message: Good work! You understand basic shapes nicely. Let's explore 3D shapes in daily life, learn about faces, edges, vertices, discover angles, and identify shapes all around us.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in shape knowledge. Don't worry! We will start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need, then move on to 3D shapes and angles.

low_score.message: You need to review basic shapes, sides, and corners first. We have created a playlist with fun videos to build a strong foundation. After watching, you can retake the quiz and then learn about 3D shapes and angles.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how geometry concepts build on each other

concepts: 3D shape names (sphere, cube, cylinder, cone, pyramid)
Identifying 2D and 3D shapes in the real world

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 70% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

shapeIdentification.description: % of students who can correctly name common 3D shapes and identify them in daily life

faceEdgeVertexCounting.description: % of students who can correctly count faces, edges, and vertices of basic 3D shapes

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 30-35 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 35-40 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 45-50 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (15 min) + retake + main content (25 min) = 45-55 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.solution1: Use real objects: hold a ball (sphere) and draw a circle on paper. Emphasize that a sphere takes up space, a circle is flat
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students struggling to count faces on 3D shapes
commonChallenges.solution2: Use physical models or nets (unfolded shapes) to count faces one by one
commonChallenges.solution3: Start with the corner of a book (right angle) and use hands to show bigger and smaller angles
